summary: Monster Truckin'→Weather Report jam, soaring Eyes‑Dew finale
blurb: The second set shines with a monster Truckin' → Weather Report Suite Prelude → Wharf Rat sequence that rolls into an expansive Eyes → Morning Dew climax. Jerry's tone and solo work are on fire while Keith’s keyboards lift the jams. Highlights also include Candyman, Box of Rain and Here Comes Sunshine.
review: Baltimore Civic Center, March 26, 1973 – This show is a textbook example of the Dead at their improvisational peak. The first set rolls along nicely with classics like "Box of Rain" and a crisp "China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider", but it’s the second set that steals the night. Kicking off with a spirited "Ramble On Rose" and "Here Comes Sunshine", the band builds momentum into a legendary "Truckin' → Weather Report Suite Prelude → Wharf Rat" stretch – Jerry cooking on the jam, Keith adding two‑cents of sparkle, and Bill’s chugging groove keeping everything tight. The momentum never quits, soaring through "Eyes Of The World > Morning Dew" to an emotional finale that leaves the audience buzzing. Candyman gets a killer solo, and Phil’s bass adds conviction throughout, especially in the transitions into "Morning Dew". Across both top‑tier Charlie Miller soundboard transfers the performance with crystal‑clear fidelity, making this one of the must‑listen shows from the spring ’73 run.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Both recordings are marked positive, repeatedly praising the same highlights – the Truckin' > Weather Report > Wharf Rat jam, Eyes‑Dew climax, and standout solos. No poor songs or negative comments appear, indicating strong consensus of an exceptional performance.

best_recording.reason: Excellent Charlie Miller soundboard transfer – crisp, hi‑fidelity mix with no dropouts, capturing the full set and all key jams.

key_highlights: Monster Truckin' → Weather Report Suite Prelude → Wharf Rat jam that defines the second set
Soaring "Eyes Of The World" into a powerful "Morning Dew" climax
Candyman receives a smoking solo, showcasing Jerry’s tone and vocal depth
Keith’s keyboards add lift throughout the improvisations

band_performance.Jerry: Passionate, clean playing; cooking on the jam, superb solo at the Truckin' bridge and a fantastic tone on the Candyman solo.
band_performance.Phil: Powerful bass with conviction on Eyes, high notes into Morning Dew, strong backing vocals and "Phil zone" energy during Playing In The Band.
band_performance.Bob: Solid rhythm work and vocal interplay (e.g., El Paso), keeping the groove steady.
band_performance.Keith: On big time; great backing during jams, his two‑cents lift the music and add sparkle to the improvisations.
band_performance.Billy: Single‑drummer groove solid and chugging nicely, holding the band tight through long improvisations.
